_what: VERDICT 088 fixture — idea-engine PROPOSAL 075 (the hint that hides — max_depth hint visibility clip). Every value copied verbatim from the P075 outbox block / idea file (ideas/superbot-mineverse/max-depth-hint-visibility-clip-2026-07-15.md @ idea-engine main 87bbb39, PR #439 merged 2026-07-15T11:09:44Z; outbox block stamped 2026-07-15T11:06:24Z). Committed BEFORE the runner (born-red card -> fixtures -> runner -> accepted run).

superbot_mineverse.reverified_firsthand: this slice, on a read-only PUBLIC-HTTPS shallow clone landing exactly at the pinned HEAD (the Q-0272 / P073-P075 transport precedent — the local git proxy may fail for fleet repos; public HTTPS worked, as it did for the drafter). Every pin below matched verbatim BEFORE this fixture was written. A scratch REHEARSAL additionally drove the ACTUAL committed server/views.py + server/snapshot_validation.py over the full 80-cell lattice, the T3 witness, the G5 hand world, and the three G6 repair worlds: every disclosed census reproduced (ALL PASS, exit 0). The committed sim below is an INDEPENDENT hermetic re-implementation and never reads that clone.

hint_optional.text: max_depth — OPTIONAL world-shape hint: deepest reachable depth index. Oracle-confirmed depth bands are 0-3. Consumers must fall back gracefully when absent. — type integer, minimum 0, maximum 3; NOT in the envelope's required tuple

miner_depth.text: depth — Current depth index (0 = surface). Oracle-confirmed depth bands: 0-3. — integer, minimum 0, maximum 3, REQUIRED

miner_record_depth.text: record_depth — Deepest depth ever reached by this miner (same 0-3 bands). — integer, minimum 0, maximum 3, REQUIRED

applicator_inventory.note: ZERO cross-field/conditional applicator KEYS anywhere in the schema (the only textual hit on 'not' is prose inside the schema_version description, line 11); the committed validator is a generic schema interpreter (server/snapshot_validation.py::validate_snapshot, :316) — bounds, required tuples, types; no cross-field rule exists

contract_table_row.text: `max_depth` | integer 0-3 | no | World-shape hint: deepest reachable depth index (oracle-confirmed bands 0-3).

ladder_ghost_elif.text: if miner.get("depth") == depth: here.append(name)  elif miner.get("record_depth") == depth: record_only.append(name) — the elif is PER-BAND, not per-miner: a live miner at band d also gets a record chip at band r != d

minimap_band_filter.text: if miner.get("depth") != depth: continue — a miner plots (points) or lists (unplotted) ONLY inside its own band's panel; a band past max_depth is never rendered, so its miners appear NOWHERE on the minimap

badge_equality.text: if isinstance(miner.get("record_depth"), int) and miner["record_depth"] == max_depth: earned.append("deep_diver")

badge_catalog_description.text: deep_diver — Deep Diver — record depth equals the world's max depth

absence_fallback.text: max_depth = snapshot.get("max_depth"); if not isinstance(max_depth, int): max_depth = depth_max()  # schema fallback when the hint is absent — depth_max() reads the schema's miner depth maximum = 3

fallback_pin_test.text: No max_depth hint -> the schema's depth bound backstops the ladder.

honest_listing_doctrine.text: minimap unplotted: names 'listed honestly, never silently dropped' (scoped to position-malformed miners at a RENDERED band); shape_gear: 'renders an honest empty slot instead of hiding it'; shape_position: an honest 'position unknown' instead of plotting garbage — honest-visibility is the committed house style of this exact file

served_every_request.text: The file is re-read fresh and v1-validated on EVERY request — schema-valid = rendered; the one load-validate-or-503 path

ingest_persists.text: MINING_SNAPSHOT_PATH — the persist target: the SAME live-feed file the read seam re-reads fresh and v1-validates on every request, so an accepted snapshot is served on the very next read

ordering_no_sequence_key.text: Last-write-wins, replaced whole … v1 carries no sequence key

frontend_meter.text: bar.style.width = `${world.max_depth ? (miner.depth / world.max_depth) * 100 : 0}%` — integer 0 is JS-falsy, so a valid m = 0 hint renders every meter at 0%

frontend_ladder.text: renderLadder renders only the served bands ('bands 0-3, current + record markers'); record chips are 'Name · record'

leaderboards_unclipped.text: build_leaderboards ranks ALL miners (depth board key (depth, mining XP); coins board key (coins,)) — the boards never read max_depth, which is what lets a world-view-invisible miner rank #1

miner_template_pinned.source: data/sample_snapshot.json miners[0] (DeepDelver) @ b9ade33 — the pinned sample-derived valid values the registration holds all non-lattice miner fields at

_grounding.harvest_anomalies: ZERO — every pinned constant and quoted line matched verbatim at superbot-mineverse b9ade33, the applicator sweep came back EMPTY, and the scratch rehearsal against the actual committed modules reproduced every disclosed census

committed_constants.m_eff_law: m_eff = 3 when the hint is absent (or non-int), else m — the committed fallback is reserved for ABSENCE (views.py:633-634), never for out-of-range miners
committed_constants.lattice: hint m in {absent, 0, 1, 2, 3} x depth d in 0..3 x record r in 0..3, one miner per document, all other miner fields held at the pinned template values; multi-miner interactions are linear (per-miner shaping), pinned by the T3 two-miner witness

laws_registered.ghost: record chip iff r <= m_eff AND r != d (per-BAND elif — a drafting-run correction to the drafter's first hand-hypothesis, disclosed in the registration)
laws_registered.map: minimap presence (points or unplotted) iff d <= m_eff
laws_registered.fully_invisible: no here row, no record_only row, no minimap points, not even unplotted — iff d > m_eff AND r > m_eff
laws_registered.note: the pinned template carries a valid position, so the unplotted list is empty on every lattice cell — unplotted can only name a miner whose BAND is rendered (views.py:320-326), which is exactly why it cannot rescue a clipped miner

censuses_registered.ghost_only_closed_form: (3 - m) * (m + 1) summed over present hints — a stale 'Name · record' chip at a shallow band is the ONLY trace of a miner whose live position renders nowhere
censuses_registered.badge_flip_def: cells (m in {0,1,2}, d in 0..3) where raising the record from r = m to r = m + 1 — digging ONE band deeper — LOSES deep_diver
censuses_registered.badge_overshoot_def: cells with hint present and r > m: the record strictly EXCEEDS the world's stated max and the badge is denied
censuses_registered.m0_inversion: at m = 0 the never-dug newcomer (r = 0) EARNS Deep Diver while the r = 3 explorer is denied it
censuses_registered.t4_domination: over all 64 (m present, d, r) comparisons against the absent-hint twin: visibility(absent) >= visibility(m) EVERYWHERE, strictly greater on exactly the 14 fully-invisible cells — the optional field can only remove miners from the world views, never add one

t3_witness.document: m = 2; miner A (d = 3, r = 3, coins 59999, xp.game_total 9999 — top coins/XP); miner B (d = 2, r = 2, template values); all other fields at the pinned template
t3_witness.expected: ACCEPTED by the re-derived validator; A is #1 on the depth board AND #1 on the coins board while appearing in ZERO ladder bands and ZERO minimap panels (not even unplotted); A is NOT deep_diver while the strictly shallower B IS; omitting the hint from the SAME document flips all three at once (A visible, A badged, B unbadged)

five_facts: Q(1,0) ghost chip 'Q · record' only (r = 0 band; not live there)
deep_diver = {P, Q} — the never-dug pair earns 'deepest', the two divers are denied

a_conformance_applicator.rule: require d <= m AND r <= m when the hint is present
a_conformance_applicator.censuses: every invisibility census ZERO and zero surviving flip pairs — REJECT flips to APPROVE, live

b_overflow_bands.rule: bands = range(max(m_eff, deepest miner d or r) + 1)

c_badge_gte.byte_identity: at m_eff = 3, r >= 3 is equivalent to r == 3 (r <= 3 by schema) — BYTE-IDENTICAL to the committed == law; today's sample sits exactly ON the degeneracy cell (sample m = 3), which is why the sample calibration cannot see the bug

frontend_meter_reporting.law: width% = (d / m) * 100 when m truthy, else 0 (falsy-zero guard, app.js:1173)
frontend_meter_reporting.note: REPORTING only — the decision numbers are all server-side

REJECT.R1: the fully-invisible census over the accepted lattice is >= 1 (expected exactly 14) AND every such cell is verified absent from here, record_only, points, AND unplotted — genuinely unlisted anywhere in the served world views
REJECT.R2: the badge non-monotonicity census is >= 1 (expected exactly 12 flip cells), with the overshoot-denial census attached (expected 24)
REJECT.R3: hint-domination holds (T4: absence >= presence on all 64 comparisons, strict somewhere) — supplying the optional field is never safer and somewhere strictly worse

decision_rule.APPROVE: fully-invisible census = 0 AND flip census = 0 over the accepted lattice — mutually exclusive with REJECT by arithmetic

NULL_axes.n1_conformance: if the pinned schema/validator text carries a cross-field rule binding d, r <= m (this fixture carries the schema constants and the applicator inventory; the sweep is the check), the drop cells are non-conforming input and the finalized finding is the corrected conformance boundary
NULL_axes.n2_acceptance: if the sim's re-derived validator disagrees with the pinned validator semantics on any lattice cell, the corrected acceptance table IS the finding
NULL_axes.n3_intent: if a committed mineverse sentence ratifies the hint as authoritative-not-hint (none found at drafting OR at this slice's firsthand re-verification — the fixture carries the claim-1 and claim-3 quotes to check against), direction signed: REJECT downgrades to a documentation gap on the same numbers, disclosed not ruled

gates.G1_twin_arms: Arm B equals Arm A on every census, every closed form, every cell verdict (exact integers); Arm F equals Arm B cell-by-cell on all 80 x 4 outputs (live, ghost, map, badge)
gates.G2_acceptance: all 80 lattice cells + the T3 witness pass the re-derived schema check (bounds, required fields, types), and the applicator inventory of the pinned schema is empty of conditional/cross-field keywords
gates.G3_censuses: fully-invisible {9, 4, 1, 0} = (3-m)^2, total 14; minimap-invisible 24; ghost-only 10; badge censuses 20 / 12 / 24; all exact
gates.G4_laws: live iff d <= m_eff; ghost iff r <= m_eff and r != d; map iff d <= m_eff; deep_diver iff r == m_eff; T4 domination on all 64 comparisons with strictness exactly on the 14
gates.G5_hand_world: the five pencil-checkable facts (fixture g5_hand_world) verified against the rendered structures
gates.G6_consequence_battery: repair world (a) 80 -> 46 accepted, all censuses zero, zero flip pairs; (b) overflow bands invisible 0/80; (c) >= badge 44/80, zero flips, byte-identical to == at m_eff = 3

must_equal_rows_registered_contacts: fully-invisible census = 14 with per-hint {9, 4, 1, 0} = (3-m)^2 (closed form vs enumeration, exact)
minimap-invisible = 24; ghost-only = 10 (closed forms vs enumeration, exact)
strict-domination count = 14 EXACTLY (T4 strictness lands on precisely the fully-invisible cells)

the m_eff = 3 byte-identity: (r == 3) equals (r >= 3) on every r in 0..3 — the registered margin-0 contact (iii): today's world sits exactly ON the degeneracy cell where the bug has no observable consequence
knife_edge_rows_two_sided: (i) d = m_eff is the LAST visible band: live at d = m_eff, invisible-live at d = m_eff + 1 (checked at every present hint m < 3) — the entire drop census sits one integer past an exact contact
(ii) deep_diver's == is itself a two-sided margin-0 law: r = m earns; r = m - 1 and r = m + 1 do not (checked at every m where the neighbour exists)
(iv) the m = 0 meter row rides the falsy-zero boundary: 0 is a valid hint AND JavaScript-falsy — modeled as the committed ternary, reporting-only
margin_ledger.policy: the V086 typed-ledger convention: must-equal rows (registered contacts) gate as two-sided EQUALITIES; the knife-edge rows are REGISTERED margin-0 contacts (the head's thesis), gated as the exact two-sided laws above; no UNregistered decision comparison sits at margin 0

arm_R.role: REPORTING-ONLY — seeded random fully-valid documents; rates of drop/ghost/badge events under uniform sampling; NO statistical gate rides Arm R
arm_R.document_model: k ~ uniform{1..7} miners per document; hint ~ uniform{absent, 0, 1, 2, 3}; per miner d ~ uniform{0..3}, r ~ uniform{0..3}, other fields at the pinned template (suid uniquified)
arm_R.draw_discipline: random.Random(seed); draws counted and asserted (k, hint, d, r per miner); per-miner events evaluated by the Arm B evaluator; ties/order presentation-only
arm_R.disclosed_reporting_landing: NONE — the registration discloses the estimand (rates of drop/ghost/badge events) and the seed set only, no seeded counts; the empirical rates print beside the exact expectations and are not compared to any disclosed value (anomaly-census coverage: NOT-DISCLOSED, the V087 structured-census convention)

roles.20261673: aux, reserved, NEVER read (the P054-P074 aux convention)

seeds.provenance: the drafter's registered allocation IS the session seed set (V077-V087 precedent); 20261660-663 are P074/V087's registered set; the gap 20261665-669 is the drafter's disclosed in-flight buffer, unused; sweep of this clone at origin/main d6610dc maxes at genuine seed 20261663 — the lone in-tree hit on the digit-string 20261664 is a DATA numeral (0.00202616643 in verdict-032 results.json), not a seed

expected_landing_disclosed: REJECT (the sim re-derives from scratch and must not trust these): R1 census 14 (per-hint {9, 4, 1, 0}), all 14 verified unlisted on all four surfaces; R2 flips 12 + overshoot 24 + the m = 0 inversion; R3 domination with strictness on exactly the 14. Repairs RAN at drafting: conformance applicator 80 -> 46 all-zero; overflow bands 0/80; >= badge 44/80 zero flips with the m_eff = 3 byte-identity. The REJECT indicts the unvalidated cross-field trust and the exact-equality badge law — never the hint field itself (world shape is real data), never the fallback (correct and pinned by the repo's own test), never the game

falsifiability_worlds.g6_repairs: all three G6 repair worlds flip the corresponding REJECT clause live (a: APPROVE on the 46-cell lattice; b: R1 dies; c: R2 dies with today's sample semantics provably untouched)
falsifiability_worlds.n1_n3_axes: a cross-field applicator or an authoritative-hint sentence anywhere in the pinned tree kills the head — drafting swept and found neither; this slice's firsthand re-verification swept again and found neither
falsifiability_worlds.n2_axis: any disagreement between the re-derived acceptance and the pinned validator semantics is the finding, not a re-run request

conventions.C1: all decision arithmetic exact small integers; no floats in any decision path (the meter rows are reporting-only and use the committed JS ternary semantics)
conventions.C2: Arm B is INDEPENDENTLY WRITTEN: a dict-of-cells exhaustive enumerator evaluating each (m, d, r) cell by direct predicate scans over explicitly constructed band/panel index sets — no shared code with Arm A's closed forms and no shared code with Arm F's verbatim transcription; the second decision evaluator reads Arm B's cell table only
conventions.C3: disclosed-anchor comparison: every registration numeral (censuses, closed forms, witness facts, repair-world numbers, meter rows) is a registered GATE; any mismatch between a re-derived value and a disclosed numeral raises a FIRST-CLASS ANOMALY, never a silent gate
conventions.C4: the falsifiability worlds are named registration cells (the three G6 repair worlds; the n-axes); the APPROVE-liveness check confirms the branch fires in repair world (a) WITHOUT adding a decision path beyond the registered clause
conventions.C5: typed margin ledger (the V086 convention): must-equal rows two-sided; the three registered knife-edges gated as their exact two-sided laws; no UNregistered decision comparison at margin 0
conventions.C6: Arm R estimand + draw discipline pinned (document model above); draw counts asserted; NO statistical gate
conventions.C7: seed constructor registry: exactly 20261670, 20261671, 20261672 constructed, in that order; aux 20261673 asserted never read; registry high-water 20261673 (reserved; highest read 20261672)
conventions.C8: print discipline: every registered quantity is a small integer; gates compare in memory FIRST and print second
conventions.C9: canonical serialization: sorted keys, no timestamps in results.json or stdout (byte-identity across process runs)
conventions.C10: Arm F is the FIDELITY leg: the committed band loop, per-band elif, minimap band filter, badge equality, and absence fallback transcribed verbatim from the pinned quotes, run over real document dicts; it must equal Arm B cell-by-cell — a divergence is INVALID (G1), and a joint divergence of both from the registration is the n2 finding
conventions.C11: anomaly census is STRUCTURED per the V087 convention (coverage line): per disclosed-numeral class, report compared-and-matched (count), compared-and-mismatched (anomalies), and NOT-DISCLOSED (vacancy) — Arm R's seeded rates are the vacancy class this slice
conventions.C12: APPROVE/REJECT mutual exclusivity verified as arithmetic (census = 0 vs census >= 1 on the same enumeration), disclosed here before the runner
conventions.C13: exit contract: exit 0 iff all self-checks pass and exactly one ruling token issues; anomalies do not affect the exit code
